text:
Contentions of the parties
3. 
Mr. P. N. Ravindran, learned senior counsel for the appellant, argued 
that both the Tribunal in allowing the original applications as well 
as the High Court in dismissing the writ petitions fell in error in 
not appreciating that a higher qualification could never have been 
regarded as a disqualification for appointment on the post of Lascar. 
4. 
Mr. Ravindran relied on the decisions in Parvaiz Ahmed Parry v. 
State of Jammu and Kashmir10 and Chandra Shekhar Singh and 
Others v. State of Jharkhand 11 in support of the contention that a 
candidate having a higher degree in the subject prescribed under 
the advertisement cannot be disqualified by reason of ineligibility for 
not possessing the required degree.
5. 
Mr. Ravindran further argued that by the time the Tribunal was moved 
by the unsuccessful candidates, the appellant had not been appointed. 
However, he did figure in the “Ranked List”. During the pendency of
